CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100172209) FILED BY DEFENDANT SONIKA CORPORATION JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE DEC-29-2022 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF VIET "DAVID" HOANG CHUNG'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT ON STIPULATION AGAINST DEFENDANT SONIKA CORPORATION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PATTY TO ALSO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A SEPARATE PROPOSED JUDGMENT. IF THE PARTIES ARE IN AGREEMENT THAT THE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT SHOULD BE DELAYED TWO WEEKS, THE PREVAILING PARTY MAY WAIT TWO WEEKS TO SUBMIT THE SEPARATE PROPOSED JUDGMENT TO THE COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2023 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-02-2023 Continued To Mar-28-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Entry Of Judgment On Stipulation Against Defendant Sonika Corporation Declaration Of Kathryn D.Z. Domin In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-29-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KATHYRN D.Z. DOMIN, Esq. of Rutan & Tucker, LLP (Tel: 714-641-5100) for plaintiff;
Atty. CHARLIE W. YU, Esq. of Essential Law Group, PC (Tel: 415-349-5180) for defendant Sonika Corporation.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF VIET "DAVID" HOANG CHUNG's Motion For Entry Of Judgment On Stipulation Against Defendant Sonika Corporation.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Viet "David" Hoang's motion for entry of judgment on stipulation against Defendant Sonika Corporation is granted. Judgment shall be entered in Plaintiff's favor against Defendant Sonika Corporation in the amount of $282,525.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Counsel for the prevailing patty to also prepare and submit a separate proposed judgment. If the parties are in agreement that the entry of judgment should be delayed two weeks, the prevailing party may wait two weeks to submit the separate proposed judgment to the Court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
